name: DropdownOptions
description: Generic action menu triggered by an icon Button. Body is a List with ListItem rows — supports labels, dividers, and drill-in submenus rendered adjacent to the main panel.

props.name: children
props.description: Menu content — typically List + ListItem components

props.name: align
props.description: Horizontal alignment of the panel relative to trigger

props.name: side
props.description: Which side of the trigger to open on

props.name: triggerIcon
props.description: Custom trigger icon (defaults to 3-dot kebab — lucide MoreVertical)

props.name: triggerLabel
props.description: Accessible label for the trigger button

props.name: open
props.description: Controlled open state. When provided, the component is in controlled mode and the consumer must keep the state in sync via onOpenChange. Use this when a child action needs to dismiss the menu programmatically (e.g. a DropdownFilters chip handoff).

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Called whenever the menu wants to change its open state (trigger click, outside click, Escape). Required in controlled mode.

states.rationale: The dropdown panel is a passive floating surface. Interactive states live on the trigger Button (hybrid — soft variant uses OKLCH L-shift) and on the composed ListItem children (overlay hover, info-subtle fill on selected). The panel renders in a React portal positioned via the trigger wrapper's bounding rect.

keyboard.Tab: Move focus to the trigger button

accessibility.focusManagement: Trigger is a native <button> with native focus and aria-expanded tracking open state. Panel is rendered in a portal with role="menu". Closes on click outside (mousedown listener on wrapper + portal) or Escape. ListItem children own their own keyboard / focus contracts.

propertyMapping.note: The menu body — typically a <List> with <ListItem> children (type=label / divider / text / with-slots).
Defaults to lucide MoreVertical. Override with any lucide icon for context-specific triggers (Ellipsis, Settings, Filter, etc.).

useWhen: Overflow / 'more' menu on a row, card, toolbar, or table top bar
Contextual action menu with 2–7 actions that should not crowd the surface
Settings / customization entry point that drills into submenus (Columns / Filters / Export)
doNotUseFor: Selection lists with multi-select → use Dropdown / DropdownAutocomplete
Free-form popovers without an items list → use Dialog or Tooltip

knownDeviations.description: Spec mentions --dls-color-component-dropdown-options-* and --dls-radius-component-dropdown-options tokens but they do not exist in tokens.json. CSS uses semantic surface/border tokens directly and reuses --dls-radius-component-list for the panel radius.

The Figma dropdown-options frame contains the panel only — the trigger is part of the host pattern. In code, the trigger is rendered inside the same React component for ergonomics; pass triggerIcon and triggerLabel to customize.
